title: Problem-Driven Design Patterns · Behavior Case: Oop Repository Pattern
summary: Add pricing, notifications, imports, commands, and persistence without turning patterns into ceremony. Planned artifact: Add and verify the oop repository pattern increment in an extensible library operations toolkit. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use a repository boundary when domain collection semantics and persistence replacement justify abstraction.
Represent operations as command values when queuing, logging, undo, authorization, or scheduling justifies it.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
